title: Evangelion platinum collection region 4
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion platinum collection region 4             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Fri Jun 07, 2013 11:42 pm Ok so hi guys and gals, i'm a newbie on evageek's and i'm not sure if this is against the terms and conditions as the page 404'd when i went to check, so anyway i live in Australia and have been furiously hunting down a region 4 evangelion platinum boxed set, if anyone knows where i could find one for sale or even if someone has one they wouldnt mind parting with, please post here and we can discuss an arrangement, i unfortunately missed the 2006 release of it here with madman entertainment thanks for your time fellas

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Sat Jun 08, 2013 2:24 am I've seen other region DVDs, but not region 4. I'm having a bit of trouble looking for a region 4 box set as well. I know of sets of "4 episodes per DVD", but no complete box sets. Good luck, it's hell to find DVDs that have been out of print since 2006.

[]: mmmm             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Sat Jun 08, 2013 2:35 am yea its a bastard of a task, i have the original vhs set, but hunting down the platinum edition dvd box set or even just the original version's on dvd for region 4 is no easy task, cant wait for evangelion 3.33 to be released through madman entertainment, its a shame the jp release is raw and doesn't have english subs :(

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jun 08, 2013 2:40 am Wouldn't it be easier and quicker to convert a DVD player for region-free operation? That also covers things that have never been produced in more than one region.

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Sat Jun 08, 2013 2:55 am Could be a worth while investment, thanks for the advice, its not so much that it was never produced for region 4 though, its just rare is all :(

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Sat Jun 08, 2013 3:18 am I would have bought a different region DVD ages ago, since my DVD player can play all regions. I'd like to lend it to friends, though, so I don't really want a different region DVD unless I'm desperate.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jun 08, 2013 4:02 am If you're really desperate you can download the ISO files online (use google) and then play them using your computer. Although they are fairly huge (47.8 GB).

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Jun 09, 2013 10:07 pm ...I live in Australia and have been furiously hunting down a region 4 evangelion platinum boxed set.

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Sun Jun 09, 2013 10:18 pm i check daily for them on ebay, price isnt really an issue for me, not when i want something bad enough anyway ha ha, but last one that sold was on may 13th, such a shame madman produced such a limited quantity, ive considered buying the region 1 boxed set and then stripping the region code of the disks to make it playable on any device but i dno, having the actual madman edition would be much cooler

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Jun 09, 2013 11:54 pm I know how you feel. It was only early last year that I finally got my hands on R4 D+R and EOE releases, which I got from buying from someone else who was kind enough to sell over on the Madboards. Looks like you going to have to do the same with Platinum, or wait it out on eBay. Luck dude.

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Mon Jun 10, 2013 12:39 am yea thanks for the advice man , glad i found this forum

[]:             Quote Post by tootiredtoriot » Mon Jun 10, 2013 11:21 pm Blue Monday wrote: Late to the game unfortunately - Though if you do keep your eyes on eGay, R4 Platinum sets pop up all the time to varying prices. I have a mate that usual lets me know when a good one comes up because he knows how much I hate the MM 10th Anniversary version I have, and have been wanting the regular fatpack for a while now. And yeah; stoked for MM 3.33 BD release, haha.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Jun 10, 2013 11:25 pm Ugly border art, yo. Detracts from the overall presentation and looks shitty compared to the regular box set and fatpack. But hey, still better than nothing right?

[]:             Quote Post by Noobsbane » Tue Jun 11, 2013 2:54 am haha id be happy to own either at this point Q.Q

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sat Jun 15, 2013 2:24 am I'm not even going to bother looking for a MM Eva boxset I'm just going to save up for a US PS4 and then hunt down a copy of the R1 version. Tis the perfect plan.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Jun 16, 2013 5:26 am No need to "hunt" - RightStuf still have the R1 thinpack in-stock: http://www.rightstuf.com/1-800-338-6827/catalogmgr/HE=8yi-nBgHEw5AixM/browse/item/67745/4/0/0

[]:             Quote Post by steve_stevenson » Mon Jun 17, 2013 11:50 pm someone listed the platinum box set on Australian ebay today. not mine, it just turned up in my daily Evangelion search.
